gaius_norbanus_convo_2_n01.npcDialogue: Shut the door. Bolt it. What I'm about to say stays in this room or I'll gut you myself, friend or not. Sulla's been stripped of his command — Sulpicius rammed the vote through the tribal assembly. The command goes to Marius. And Sulla's answer — he's marching his legions on Rome. Not a rumor. Not bluster. Six legions moving north from Capua as we speak.

responses.playerDialogue: How long before they reach the gates?
A Roman general marching on Rome. In five hundred years of the Republic, this has never happened. Not once.
Who else knows? More importantly — who's already slipped out to join him?
Can it still be stopped? Is there any space left to talk?

fallbackResponse.playerDialogue: Tell me everything. All of it.

gaius_norbanus_convo_2_n02.npcDialogue: Two days. Three if Jupiter loves us. His own officers sent a delegation to talk him down — he had them beaten with rods. The senate dispatched praetors to order his return. He didn't even read the letter. This isn't a political maneuver, Crispus. This is a Roman army invading Rome. Marius is scraping together a defense — veterans, urban poor, whatever gladiators we can drag out of the ludus. Against six trained legions. So I need to know where you stand. Not tomorrow. Now.

responses.playerDialogue: Where I've always stood. Right here. Give me my orders.
Sulla broke the law. I stand with the Republic — whatever's left of it. What do you need?
Two days is enough time to make the right choice — not just the loud one. Let me think.

fallbackResponse.playerDialogue: What are the options? Lay them out.

gaius_norbanus_convo_2_n03.npcDialogue: Half the senate's already packed for their country villas — the smart half. The rest are composing orations about sacred tradition while a man with thirty thousand swords walks toward them. Negotiation's done. It ended the moment Sulla gave the marching order. This is about survival now — ours, and whatever's left of the Republic to save.

options.playerDialogue: Then stop telling me what's over and tell me what's next.

gaius_norbanus_convo_2_n04.npcDialogue: Here's the shape of it. Marius is positioning our forces at the Esquiline Gate. Sulpicius is whipping the popular assemblies into something resembling courage. And I've got orders to hand out — real orders, from the old man himself. You're with us, you take a sector of the defense and you hold it. You're not — and I say this once, because I won't say it again — now's the time to walk. Once those legions hit the walls, nobody walks anywhere.

options.playerDialogue: Give me a sector. I'll hold it or they'll find me dead on it.
No. I won't bleed for a lost cause — Sulla's the future and I intend to be alive in it. I'm going to his camp.

gaius_norbanus_convo_2_n05.npcDialogue: The Subura approach — it's narrow, it's ugly, and the locals will fight beside you because they've got nowhere else to run. Hold it as long as the gods allow. When the line breaks — and it will break — fall back to the Esquiline and find Marius. If you can't find Marius, get out of the city and make for Africa. He'll regroup there. This might be the last time we talk as free men, Crispus. It's been an honor. Truly. Now go.

gaius_norbanus_convo_2_n06.npcDialogue: Then go. Fast — before I remember what I'm supposed to do to deserters. I won't pretend I don't understand the arithmetic. Sulla's got legions. We've got old men and kitchen knives. The math is the math. But I want you to carry one thing with you to his camp, since you won't be carrying your honor. Every man in this city who dies tomorrow — every freedman, every baker's boy with a borrowed spear — dies because men like you decided winning mattered more than being right. Now get out of my sight before I change my mind about letting you leave it.

operations.reason: Stood with Marius when Sulla marched on Rome
Sulla's forces mark you as an enemy combatant
The senate moderates consider you a Marian radical

deltas.reason: Honored your commitment when betrayal was easier

operations.reason: Deserted the Marian cause at the moment of crisis
Defected to Sulla's side before the battle — useful but distrusted

deltas.reason: Switched sides under pressure — a gambler's move
